contributingFactors: RAM-backed emptyDir volume (medium: Memory, sizeLimit: 256Mi) acts as a tmpfs sink — writes consume physical node RAM rather than disk
The 'leaker' container runs an unbounded write loop (dd if=/dev/urandom of=/data/leak_$i bs=1M count=1 every 5 seconds) with no termination condition
emptyDir sizeLimit (256Mi) exceeds the leaker container memory limit (192Mi), meaning the container will be OOMKilled before the volume limit triggers
Both pods tolerate node memory pressure (node.kubernetes.io/memory-pressure: NoSchedule), preventing eviction under node memory pressure
Configuration regression detected: current Deployment spec hash matches pre-remediation state — a prior fix (effectiveness 0.75) was reverted or overwritten
Two replicas spread across dev-worker-1 and dev-worker-2 means both nodes are simultaneously affected
rootCauseAnalysis.summary: The Deployment leaky-app in demo-memory-leak contains a misconfigured sidecar container 'leaker' that continuously writes 1 MiB of random data every 5 seconds to a RAM-backed emptyDir volume (medium: Memory, sizeLimit: 256Mi), causing monotonically increasing memory consumption predicted to exhaust the container's 192 MiB limit within ~30 minutes. A configuration regression was detected: the current Deployment spec matches the pre-remediation state of a prior fix, indicating the root-cause change was reverted. No OOMKill has occurred yet, but two replicas across two nodes are simultaneously affected.

selectedWorkflow.rationale: graceful-restart-v1 is the only catalog workflow applicable to a Deployment with monotonically growing memory predicted to OOMKill. A rolling restart will immediately reset the 'leaker' container's memory to zero, preventing the imminent OOMKill and preserving service availability. Confidence is capped at 0.65 (not higher) because: (1) the root cause is a misconfiguration that will fully recur after restart — the workflow's own 'whenNotToUse' warns against applying it when the issue will recur, and (2) remediation history shows two prior remediations with effectiveness scores of 0.75 and 0.40, both followed by regression. This workflow buys time but does not address the root cause; a Deployment patch to remove the 'leaker' container and RAM-backed emptyDir volume is the definitive fix and should be escalated to the development team.

kaResponse.analysis: The Deployment leaky-app in demo-memory-leak contains a misconfigured sidecar container 'leaker' that continuously writes 1 MiB of random data every 5 seconds to a RAM-backed emptyDir volume (medium: Memory, sizeLimit: 256Mi), causing monotonically increasing memory consumption predicted to exhaust the container's 192 MiB limit within ~30 minutes. A configuration regression was detected: the current Deployment spec matches the pre-remediation state of a prior fix, indicating the root-cause change was reverted. No OOMKill has occurred yet, but two replicas across two nodes are simultaneously affected.

rootCauseAnalysis.summary: The Deployment leaky-app in demo-memory-leak contains a misconfigured sidecar container 'leaker' that continuously writes 1 MiB of random data every 5 seconds to a RAM-backed emptyDir volume (medium: Memory, sizeLimit: 256Mi), causing monotonically increasing memory consumption predicted to exhaust the container's 192 MiB limit within ~30 minutes. A configuration regression was detected: the current Deployment spec matches the pre-remediation state of a prior fix, indicating the root-cause change was reverted. No OOMKill has occurred yet, but two replicas across two nodes are simultaneously affected.
causalChain: Signal: ContainerMemoryExhaustionPredicted — container 'leaker' in leaky-app pods is projected to exceed 192 MiB memory limit within 30 minutes
Why? The leaker container's working set memory is growing monotonically at ~12 MiB/min (confirmed by Prometheus range data: 4.5 MiB → 39.3 MiB over 3 minutes)
Why? The leaker container runs an infinite loop writing 1 MiB of random data to /data/leak_$i every 5 seconds, accumulating files without deletion
Why? The /data volume is an emptyDir with medium: Memory (RAM-backed tmpfs), so each write consumes physical node RAM rather than disk

Root cause: The Deployment spec includes a 'leaker' sidecar container with an unbounded memory-consuming write loop targeting a RAM-backed emptyDir volume — this is either an intentional test workload left running in a staging environment or a misconfigured sidecar that must be removed from the Deployment spec

dueDiligence.targetAccuracy: The remediation target is Deployment/leaky-app because its container spec defines the leaker container and the RAM-backed emptyDir volume. Removing the leaker container definition and/or changing the emptyDir medium from Memory to '' (disk-backed) would resolve the memory exhaustion. The pods are downstream symptoms; the Deployment spec is the configuration that needs changing.
dueDiligence.proportionality: Remediation targets the Deployment (2 replicas) which is the exact scope of the problem. No other workloads in other namespaces are affected. The fix (remove leaker container or change emptyDir medium) is a precise, targeted change to the Deployment spec.
dueDiligence.scopeCompleteness: Investigated: both pods (bzxpd and w2nsh), Deployment spec, both nodes (dev-worker-1 via describe, dev-worker-2 indirectly via Prometheus labels), Prometheus memory metrics (working set, spec limits, OOM events, usage ratio), events for both pods, remediation history. Did not investigate: dev-worker-2 node describe (node is healthy per Prometheus and pod status), NetworkPolicies (not relevant to memory issue), HPA (no HPA present — fixed 2 replicas).
dueDiligence.causalCompleteness: Traced fully: signal → growing working set → unbounded write loop → RAM-backed emptyDir → Deployment spec misconfiguration. The root cause is the Deployment spec itself containing the leaker container definition. Cannot go deeper without understanding deployment intent (intentional demo vs. accidental).
dueDiligence.evidenceSufficiency: All claims backed by tool output: (1) Deployment spec confirmed via kubectl_describe showing leaker container command and emptyDir medium:Memory; (2) Prometheus range query confirmed monotonic memory growth; (3) Memory limit of 192Mi confirmed via container_spec_memory_limit_bytes = 201,326,592 bytes; (4) No OOMKill yet confirmed via container_oom_events_total = 0; (5) Node health confirmed via kubectl_describe node; (6) Remediation regression confirmed via get_namespaced_resource_context. Leaker container logs returned empty (no stdout output from dd command with 2>/dev/null redirect), which is consistent with expected behavior.
dueDiligence.regressionAwareness: Remediation history shows a prior Tier 1 remediation (rr-cab245311260-e1d46489, completed 2026-05-11T05:07:18Z) with effectiveness_score=0.75 that reduced memory from 685 MiB to 247 MiB. regression_detected=true and current spec hash matches pre-remediation hash, confirming the fix was reverted. A Tier 2 remediation (rr-cab245311260-69d9dd43, 2026-05-10) had effectiveness_score=0.4 and signal_resolved=false. Any new remediation must ensure the spec change is durable and not overridden by a GitOps or CI/CD pipeline.
dueDiligence.alternativeHypotheses: 1) Node memory pressure causing false alarm — ruled out: dev-worker-1 has 49.8 GiB allocatable and MemoryPressure=False; node is not under pressure. 2) Application-level memory leak in the 'app' container — ruled out: Prometheus data shows only the 'leaker' container has growing working set; 'app' container memory is stable. 3) Transient spike rather than leak — ruled out: Prometheus range data shows monotonic growth pattern consistent with the described write loop rate. 4) Volume sizeLimit preventing OOMKill — considered but ruled out: emptyDir sizeLimit (256Mi) exceeds container memory limit (192Mi), so container will OOMKill before volume limit is enforced.
dueDiligence.confidenceCalibration: 0.92: Starting at 1.0. -0.05 for memory growth appearing to plateau in the range data (values stabilized at ~39 MiB in the last 5 data points), which could indicate the leak rate slowed or the tmpfs write loop is cycling — however this is likely a scrape interval artifact and the trend remains concerning. -0.03 for not being able to confirm whether this is an intentional demo workload (namespace name 'demo-memory-leak' suggests it may be) vs. a genuine misconfiguration requiring urgent remediation.

contributingFactors: RAM-backed emptyDir volume (medium: Memory, sizeLimit: 256Mi) acts as a tmpfs sink — writes consume physical node RAM rather than disk
The 'leaker' container runs an unbounded write loop (dd if=/dev/urandom of=/data/leak_$i bs=1M count=1 every 5 seconds) with no termination condition
emptyDir sizeLimit (256Mi) exceeds the leaker container memory limit (192Mi), meaning the container will be OOMKilled before the volume limit triggers
Both pods tolerate node memory pressure (node.kubernetes.io/memory-pressure: NoSchedule), preventing eviction under node memory pressure
Configuration regression detected: current Deployment spec hash matches pre-remediation state — a prior fix (effectiveness 0.75) was reverted or overwritten
Two replicas spread across dev-worker-1 and dev-worker-2 means both nodes are simultaneously affected
